semantic.role: Defines the core identity, boundaries, and governance contract of the ArchSpine mirror system.
responsibilities: Articulate the role and purpose of the ArchSpine documentation mirror system
List the core responsibilities and maintenance scope of the system
Specify invariants, out-of-scope topics, and exclusions to prevent scope creep
Provide additional contextual sections (e.g., architecture, usage notes) as needed
outOfScope: Technical implementation details beyond role and boundary definitions
Specific command-line usage or tutorials (handled in separate reference docs)
Per-language or per-audience content variations (delegated to localized atlas pages)

English.purpose: This document serves as the authoritative contract for the ArchSpine project. It establishes what the system is designed to do (define and enforce documentation governance via mirroring) and what it explicitly will not do. By clearly stating the system's role, responsibilities, and invariants, it ensures all contributors and automated agents share a consistent understanding of the project's scope and boundaries.
English.context_and_audience: Intended for contributors, maintainers, project leads, and AI agents who need a high-level, unambiguous understanding of ArchSpine's narrative and constraints. It is the first document new team members (human or automated) should consult to grasp the fundamental purpose and limitations of the system before diving into detailed implementation or usage guides.
key_takeaways: ArchSpine is a documentation mirror and governance system that enforces structural and semantic consistency across repositories and languages.
The project document is the single source of truth for the system's raison d'être, core responsibilities, and explicit non-goals.
Invariants listed here are binding: any violation represents a drift from the project's intended design.
Sections like 'extraSections' allow flexible addition of context without breaking the contract structure.
